entry.title: SAP Billing and Revenue Innovation Management – Localization: Innovations and Demo
entry.content:
Hi SAP Community!

This blog post brings you the latest innovations, features, and demos that show how legal requirements can spark software innovation in SAP Billing and Revenue Innovation Management for localization in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2408.

We are talking about a comprehensive solution that handles subscription and usage-based models. It provides a standardized approach to recurring billing, invoicing, tax compliance, and automation.

The product is designed to optimize global tax calculation, billing, invoicing, reporting, and payment collection, while ensuring seamless compliance. Localization ensures compliance with country/region-specific regulations by connecting core business processes.

With each release, SAP introduces localized country/region versions and new functions to assist customers with tax compliance, billing, invoicing, automation, and streamlined processes. This not only helps reduce compliance risks and costs but also improves the sustainability of tax operations.

For example, in SAP S/4HANA Cloud 2408, several countries/regions and functions were delivered in the product area of SAP Billing and Revenue Innovation Management for localization.

Additional functions that were not part of the roadmap were also delivered:

One example from public cloud release 2408 innovations is electronic invoicing in Convergent Invoicing for Türkiye.

Let me show you the process flow for outgoing eInvoice for Türkiye.

I’d like to show you also how the different scenarios are handled:

To complement, I want to provide you with a demo of electronic invoicing in Convergent Invoicing for Turkey with SAP Document and Reporting Compliance. I’ll show you how the system can adapt to specific country/region-specific regulations and streamline the invoicing process.

Electronic Invoicing in Convergent Invoicing for Türkiye

For more information, I encourage you to visit the following links:

  • Road Map Explorer: Future innovations planned 
  • SAP Billing and Revenue Innovation Management Localization on SAP Help Portal:  Country/Region-Specific Functions - Overview | SAP Help Portal  
  • What’s New: What's New Viewer - SAP S/4HANA Cloud
  • Blog Post: SAP Billing and Revenue Innovation Management Localization: Sustainable Compliance for Your Recurring Revenue Business

The purpose of this post is to show you how SAP Billing and Revenue Innovation Management for SAP S/4HANA Localization is a game-changer for businesses looking to streamline their financial operations while ensuring compliance with local regulations. Stay tuned for more updates and innovations in this space!

I hope you found this blog post interesting. If so, I invite you to follow my profile and the tags SAP Document and Reporting Compliance and SAP Billing and Revenue Innovation Management. Also, feel free to leave a comment or a suggestion in the comment section.

Thank you for choosing SAP to run your business complaint with local tax authorities!

entry.title: SAP Document and Reporting Compliance: Solutions for Issuing Electronic Documents to Cloud
entry.content:
(Portuguese Version)

Hi everyone, 

In this blog post, you’ll find advice and solutions to avoid common incidents when issuing an NF-e, CT-e, NFS-e, or MDF-e documents to the cloud using the SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, outbound invoicing option for Brazil. 

Verification of Entitlement for Outbound Invoicing Option for Brazil in the SAP BTP Cockpit

Confirm with your company who the global account administrator is. Only the person who has access to the global account can generate the credentials.

If you need help, refer to User and Member Management from the SAP Business Technology Platform. If the global account administrator or user with access to the global account is no longer with the company, you must create a ticket for the BC-NEO-CIS component to request a new access.

Depending on how your solution was licensed, a second global account may have been generated. If this was not the intention, you need to check with the commercial area to review the contract. 

 Certificate Error When Sending Documents to the Cloud

Generate a certificate authority chain following the procedure mentioned on the Generating a Certificate Authority Chain page. When you upload the certificate for the first time, you don’t need to provide the Company Code and the Plant fields. Additionally, you don’t have to upload the same certificate to every SAP ERP client. The first upload process will be available for the other systems as well. 

Document Remains In Process Status 

If your NF-e document is In Process status or is not returned in a productive system, you can check the following resources to solve the issue:  

  1. Confirm that you have scheduled the jobs mentioned on the Scheduling Jobs for NF-e, CT-e, NFS-e page in your system. These jobs can be used to automate various configurations of the service and can be run periodically.  
  2. Check if SEFAZ is online and available. When SEFAZ is offline or unavailable, you can send the document by contingency. Check the Learn about Existing Contingencies in the Electronic Nota Fiscal Solution in ERP blog post (scroll down for the English version) for further information on contingency solutions. 
  3.  Check and review your customizing. On the NF-e, CT-e, NFS-e Specifics page, you can search for the customizing specific for your SAP system. 

Always keep your system updated, check the required SAP Notes 2671909 for SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, outbound invoicing option for Brazil. 

Note: the NFE_SEND_DOCUMENT_TO_CLOUD job generates numbers for the NF-e (55), CT-e (57) and NFS-e (00) models. If you need to exclude the NF-e and CT-e models from the J_BNFECALLRFC job, you can implement the EXCLUDE_NFES_FROM_BATCH method in the CL_NFE_PRINT BAdI. 

To send and receive MDF-e documents using the outbound invoicing option, you can use the CL_NFE_CLOUD_MDFE_PROCESSOR class in your own solution.

Advice for NFS-e

  • If the RPS (Recibo Provisório de Serviço) field for Outbound is not numbered, follow the SAP Knowledge Base Article 3256626 for troubleshooting information. 
  • If you need to cancel an NFS-e that was not approved, follow the SAP Knowledge Base Article 3207697 for troubleshooting information. 
  • The city of São Paulo does not permit the generation of NFS-e documents in test systems. When the NFS-e document is sent, you will receive an internal message from the outbound invoicing option for Brazil.

Other Resources 

For a step-by-step guide on how to use the SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, outbound invoicing options for Brazil, refer to the SAP Help Portal page SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, outbound invoicing option for Brazil. 

You can sign up for email alerts when new SAP Notes are released or updated in the components listed below; just follow the instructions in the SAP Knowledge Base Article 2171560. 

  • CA-GTF-CSC-EDO-BR 
  • FI-LOC-NFE-BR-IN 
  • FI-LOC-NFE-BR-OUT 
  • LOD-LH-NFE 

Use the regulatory change manager tool to find out if a solution to a legal change is planned. Take a look at SAP Knowledge Base Article 3421995 and the regulatory change manager page to understand how to use this tool. 

Have you found this blog post useful? If so, choose “Like”, share it with your colleagues, and follow me for similar content. 

You are welcome to leave your questions and read similar blog posts on the SAP Document and Reporting Compliance SAP Community. Don’t forget to follow the SAP Electronic Invoicing for Brazil (SAP Nota Fiscal Eletronica) tag and the SAP Document and Reporting Compliance Community to keep up with the latest NF-e news. 

Thank you, and until next time, 
Juliana Jarotzky

entry.title: SAP Document and Reporting Compliance: Soluções para emissão de documentos eletrônicos para a nuvem
entry.content:
(English Version)

Olá! 

Neste blog post, você encontrará recomendações e soluções para evitar incidentes comuns ao emitir documentos NF-e, CT-e, NFS-e, ou MDF-e para a nuvem usando o SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, opção de faturamento de saída para o Brasil. 

Verificação de direito para opção de faturamento de saída para o Brasil no SAP BTP Cockpit 

Confirme com sua empresa quem é a pessoa que administra a global account. Somente a pessoa que tem acesso à global account pode gerar as credenciais.  

Se precisar de ajuda, consulte a página Gerenciamento de usuários e membros do SAP Business Technology Platform. Se a pessoa que administra a global account ou o usuário com acesso à global account não estiver mais na empresa, você deverá criar um ticket para o componente BC-NEO-CIS para solicitar um novo acesso.  

Dependendo de como sua solução foi licenciada, uma segunda global account pode ter sido gerada. Se essa não foi a intenção, você precisa verificar com a área comercial da sua empresa para revisar o contrato. 

Erro de certificado ao enviar documentos para a nuvem 

Gere uma cadeia de autoridade certificadora seguindo o procedimento mencionado na página Gerar uma cadeia de autoridade certificadora. Quando você carrega o certificado pela primeira vez, não precisa fornecer os campos Company Code e Plant. Além disso, você não precisa carregar o mesmo certificado em todos os clientes SAP ERP. O primeiro processo de upload também estará disponível para os outros sistemas. 

O documento permanece no status Em Processamento 

Se o seu documento NF-e tiver o status Em Processamento ou não for devolvido em um sistema produtivo, você pode verificar os seguintes recursos para resolver o problema:  

  1. Confirme que você escalonou os jobs mencionados na página Escalonar jobs para NF-e, CT-e, NFS-e em seu sistema. Esses jobs podem ser usados para automatizar várias configurações do serviço e podem ser executados periodicamente.  
  2. Verifique se a SEFAZ está online e disponível. Quando a SEFAZ está offline ou indisponível, você pode enviar o documento por contingência. Verifique a postagem Saiba mais sobre as contingências existentes na solução de Nota Fiscal Eletrônica no ERP (role para baixo para a versão em inglês) para obter mais informações sobre soluções de contingência. 
  3. Verifique e revise seu customizing. Na página Específicos da NF-e, CT-e e NFS-e, você pode procurar o customizing específico para seu sistema da SAP. 

Mantenha sempre seu sistema atualizado, verifique a SAP Note 2671909, com as SAP Notes necessárias para SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, opção de faturamento de saída para o Brasil. 

Preste atenção: o job NFE_SEND_DOCUMENT_TO_CLOUD gera números para os modelos NF-e (55), CT-e (57) e NFS-e (00). Se você precisar excluir os modelos NF-e e CT-e do job J_BNFECALLRFC, pode implementar o método EXCLUDE_NFES_FROM_BATCH na BAdI CL_NFE_PRINT. 

Para enviar e receber documentos MDF-e utilizando a opção de faturamento de saída, você pode utilizar a classe CL_NFE_CLOUD_MDFE_PROCESSOR em sua própria solução.  

Recomendações para NFS-e

  • Se o campo RPS (Recibo Provisório de Serviço) para Saída não estiver numerado, siga o SAP Knowledge Base Article 3256626 para obter informações sobre solução de problemas. 
  • Se você precisar cancelar uma NFS-e que não foi aprovada, siga o SAP Knowledge Base Article 3207697 para obter informações sobre solução de problemas. 
  • A cidade de São Paulo não permite a geração de documentos NFS-e em sistemas de teste. Quando o documento NFS-e for enviado, você receberá uma mensagem interna da opção de faturamento de saída para o Brasil. 

Outros recursos 

Para obter um passo a passo sobre como usar o SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, opção de emissão de fatura de entrada e de faturamento de saída para o Brasil, consulte as seguintes páginas do portal SAP Help: 

  • Opção de faturamento de saída para o Brasil 
  • Opção de emissão de fatura de entrada para o Brasil 

Você pode receber alerta por email quando novas SAP Notes forem lançadas ou atualizadas nos componentes listados abaixo; basta seguir as instruções do SAP Knowledge Base Article 2171560. 

  • FI-LOC-NFE-BR-OUT 
  • FI-LOC-NFE-BR-IN 
  • CA-GTF-CSC-EDO-BR 
  • LOD-LH-NFE 

Acesse o gerenciador de modificações legais para ter uma noção do que está planejado para ser suportado. Consulte o SAP Knowledge Base Article 3421995 e a página Regulatory Change Manager para entender como usar esta ferramenta. 

Gostou desse post? Dê um “like” (kudos) e compartilhe o conteúdo com seus colegas. Sinta-se à vontade para deixar suas perguntas no SAP Community e ler posts semelhantes no blogging space do SAP Document and Reporting Compliance. 

Para acompanhar as últimas notícias da NF-e, siga a tag SAP Electronic Invoicing for Brazil (SAP Nota Fiscal Eletronica) e a comunidade SAP Document and Reporting Compliance. 

Obrigado e até a próxima, 
Juliana Jarotzky

entry.title: GRC, Trade, and Tax with SAP S/4HANA Cloud 2408
entry.content:
Hello and welcome to the release highlights for GRC, Trade and Tax with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2408. This blog contains selected highlights from International Trade, Core Tax, SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, as well as SAP Risk and Assurance Management and SAP Cloud Identity Access Governance along with their business benefits and deep-dive system demos.

To get a quick overview of our SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2408 highlights for GRC, Trade and Tax, watch this 3-minute video:

 

Topics of Covered in this Blog

International Trade

  • Integration of SAP Watch List Screening with Business Partner Master Data

Tax

  • AI-Assisted Configuration for US Tax Jurisdictions

  • SAP Document and Reporting Compliance: Mass Processing of Reporting Tasks
  • SAP Document and Reporting Compliance: Electronic Invoices in Malaysia

GRC

  • SAP Risk and Assurance Management: Automatic Issue owner Determination, Notification and Assignment

  • SAP Risk and Assurance Management: Ad-Hoc Tasks in Issue and Remediation Management

  • SAP Cloud Identity Access Governance: Enhanced Access Request Workflow

  • SAP Cloud Identity Access Governance: Fine-Grained Authorization for Privileged Access Management
  • SAP Cloud Identity Access Governance: Extension of Rule Content

 

International Trade

Integration of SAP Watch List Screening with Business Partner Master Data

SAP Watch List Screening is part of international trade compliance and checks business transactions such as sales orders and deliveries against sanctioned party lists to prevent high-risk business with sanctioned persons and organizations. As of this release, you can also screen business partners in the role of customers and suppliers. SAP Watch List Screening runs on SAP BTP and is integrated with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition. ​

Value Proposition​

  • Prevent high-risk business with sanctioned persons and organizations​
  • Assure legal compliance regarding sanctioned-party-list screening for customer and supplier master data​

Capabilities​

  • Screen names and addresses of business partners in the role of customer or supplier​
  • Call the SAP Watch List Screening application on the SAP Business Technology Platform from within SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition​
  • ‘Maintain Business Partner’ app:​
  • Add status fields representing the screening status of the addresses of business partners​
  • Manage a cumulative status field on the level of general data of the business partner​
  • Consider business partner screening results during business document creation and update

 

​Video 2: As of 2408, it is now possible to screen business partners in the role of customer or supplier with SAP Watch List Screening in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

​More Information

  • SAP Help Portal: Integration with SAP Watch List Screening

Back to top

 

Tax

AI-Assisted Configuration for US Tax Jurisdictions

This feature is currently in beta testing.

Feature Overview​

If you’re using the internal UJS tax jurisdiction solution in SAP S/4HANA Cloud, you can now maintain your jurisdiction codes and rates with just three simple steps.​

  • From a dozen steps down to three: Use a natural-language prompt to retrieve detailed information from the AI, then verify, simulate, and save your configured tax jurisdiction codes and rates. ​
  • Once you’ve configured the connection between your SAP S/4HANA Cloud system and the LLM, you can chat directly with the AI to retrieve detailed address-based tax jurisdiction information. ​
  • The LLM also returns a link to the respective state tax or revenue authority, giving you an easy way to verify your tax rate information before simulating and saving your changes. 

 

​Video 3: Currently in beta testing: The AI-Assisted Configuration for US Tax Jurisdictions

Register and experience the latest AI features in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition and influence upcoming AI developments.

More Information

  • ​Get Involved Early

Back to top

 

SAP Document and Reporting Compliance: Mass Processing of Reporting Tasks

Businesses need to create reports to be submitted to the tax authorities across countries resulting in hundreds of reports per month. Instead of creating them one by one, business users can accelerate statutory reporting using the mass processing of reporting tasks in SAP Document and Reporting Compliance.

Value Proposition

  • Reduce manual effort for creation and submission of statutory reports
  • Lower the risk of noncompliance due to missed deadlines
  • Enhance the overall business user experience by reducing user interventions required during individual report processing

Capabilities

  • Mass processing of reports by allowing reporting actions such as report run creation
  • Centralized progress log indicating the overall status of the mass action as well as details of processing and results of the action for each report
  • Ability to perform report-level checks such as authorization for each report during the mass action processing
  • Parallel processing of mass actions across reports

Fig. 1: As of SAP S/4HANA Cloud 2402, business users can accelerate statutory reporting using the mass processing of reporting tasks in SAP Document and Reporting Compliance

More Information

  • SAP Help Portal: Mass Action

Back to top

 

SAP Document and Reporting Compliance: Electronic Invoices in Malaysia

As of August 2024, Malaysia authorities are introducing mandatory electronic invoices. The country wants to promote electronic invoicing in business transactions between businesses. Electronic invoicing is a measure for digitizing business relationships, reducing transaction costs, and increasing transparency in commercial traffic.  With SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, as of 2408, you can seamlessly generate and electronically exchange invoices through authorities’ portal.

Value Proposition

  • Enable comprehensive compliance with new regulations for electronic invoices in Malaysia
  • Deliver a consistent user experience across countries and business processes
  • Simplify implementation of new local requirements
  • Automate and simplify invoicing processes

Capabilities

  • Support validation through authorities’ portal of
  • Customer invoices
  • Buyer-issued invoices
  • Refund notes
  • Buyer rejections

Fig. 2: As of SAP S/4HANA Cloud 2402, you can seamlessly generate and electronically exchange invoices through authorities’ portal in Malaysia in SAP Document and Reporting Compliance

More Information

  • SAP Help Portal: Document and Reporting Compliance

Back to top

 

GRC

SAP Risk and Assurance Management

SAP Risk and Assurance Management is integrated with SAP S/4HANA Cloud, public edition and provides you with the tools to become compliant with internal controls, laws, and regulations that apply to your organization. In Issue Management and Remediation, you can process and monitor issues resulting from manual or automated procedures in Issue Management and Remediation.

 

Automatic Issue Owner Determination, Notification and Assignment

Now, you benefit from automatic dispatching of new issues to issue owners, email notifications for issue nominees, and automatic issue owner assignments.

Value Proposition

  • Enable automatic dispatching of new issues to issue owners
  • Enable email notifications to make nominees aware of new issues
  • Avoid orphaned issues by automatic issue owner assignment after a grace period
  • Help issues find their best processor under consideration of absences, workload, and so on
  • Provide clear notification status for each issue available

Capabilities

  • Automatic determination of issue owner nominees based on business data
  • Email notifications with direct link to the issue for all nominees
  • Category-specific reminder email notification after a configurable period of time
  • Fallback mechanism to assign a default user after a configurable grace period
  • Dashboard provides easy access for issue nominees

More Information

  • SAP Help Portal: Notification of Nominees
  • SAP Help Portal: and How to Set Up Automatic Issue Owner Assignment (Admin Guide)

 

Video 3: Now, compliance specialists benefit from automatic dispatching of new issues to issue owners, email notifications for issue nominees, and automatic issue owner assignments in SAP Risk and Assurance Management

Back to top

 

Ad-Hoc Tasks in Issue Management and Remediation

As of this release, you can create user-defined tasks on ad-hoc basis and complement existing task lists.

Value Proposition

  • Get a flexible extension of task lists by issue owner​
  • Address topics using tasks that have not been foreseen at design time​
  • Avoid communication outside the Process Issues app in order to address issue-specific topics

Capabilities

Ad-hoc tasks allowing users to create tasks at runtime that are not provided through predefined task list templates:

  • Create user-defined tasks on an ad-hoc basis
  • Complement predefined task lists with tailored tasks

Video 4: Now, you can create user-defined tasks on ad-hoc basis and complement existing task lists in SAP Risk and Assurance Management

More Information

  • SAP Help Portal: Creating Tasks Manually

Back to top

 

SAP Cloud Identity Access Governance: Enhanced Access Request Workflow

SAP Cloud Identity Access Governance, access request service is a cloud service for creating self-service requests to applications for on-premise and cloud source applications and systems. Thanks to the new approach of a dynamic risk owner determinator, you can dynamically determine the current risk owner stage approver. This determinator assigns the risk owner stage approver based on business rules that evaluate both risk and user attributes when an access request is created.

Value Proposition

  • Gain additional flexibility to define a more streamlined access approval process based on various business needs

Capabilities

  • Additional optional configurations in the access request workflow:
  • Dynamically identify the risk approver in the approval process
  • Based on configuration setting allow access-risk mitigation during the approval process

Fig. 3: Thanks to the enhanced access request workflow, you can dynamically identify the risk approver in the access request approval process

More Information

  • SAP Help Portal: Dynamical Risk Owner Determinator

Back to top

 

SAP Cloud Identity Access Governance: Fine-Grained Authorization for Privileged Access Management

SAP Cloud Identity Access Governance, privileged access management service is a cloud solution that lets you create self-service requests for emergency access to systems and applications. Thanks to this innovation, you can facilitate granular authorization roles by application.

Value Proposition

  • Enhance security and control by helping ensure that users have access only to the applications necessary for their roles
  • Reduce the risk of unauthorized access and potential security breaches
  • Align with the principle of least privilege, and improve overall compliance with security policies and regulations

Capabilities

  • Granting access to each application within the privileged access management (PAM) service, through distinct roles in SAP Business Technology Platform, facilitating granular authorization roles

Fig. 4: Thanks to the fine-grained authorizations for Privileged Access Management, you can facilitate granular authorization roles by application

More Information

  • SAP Help Portal: Pre-delivered role collections on BTP

Back to top

 

SAP Cloud Identity Access Governance: Extension of Rule Content

SAP Cloud Identity Access Governance is integrated with SAP S/4HANA Cloud, public edition and supports comprehensive identity management and access governance. It allows you to minimize access risk and mitigate potential risks for financial loss.

Value Proposition

  • Support continuous compliance to access policies through provision of out of the box rule set to assess access risks
  • Upgrading applications may introduce new roles, permissions, and potential conflicts. Ensures access risk rule sets are updated to maintain alignment between access risk analysis assessments and the evolving landscape.

Capabilities

  • Updated pre-delivered rule set content for additional scenarios, including:
  • Segregation of risks
  • Critical-access risks

Fig. 5: The extension of rule content ensures that access risk rule sets are updated to maintain alignment between access risk analysis assessments and the evolving landscape

More Information

  • SAP Help Portal: What’s New

Back to top 

 

Don’t hesitate to leave a comment and check out our community page to ask your questions and engage with the experts. Follow the PSCC_Enablement tag to stay up to date with our latest blog posts. 

Follow us on @SAP and #S4HANA, and myself on LinkedIn and X.

📽️ Watch the Replays of Our SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2408 Early Release Series! 

From July 8 to July 12, we hosted a series of 20 compelling live sessions to highlight the exciting innovations shipped with the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2408 release. Missed the live sessions? We've got you covered! Take advantage of the entire series or pick and choose the sessions most relevant to you. You will find a 60-minute-long recording and a presentation for GRC, Trade and Tax as well as 19 other topics of interest. Don't miss this chance and watch them on demand – anywhere, anytime. 

🔎Find All of Our Assets 

SAP S/4HANA Cloud is the foundation of the intelligent, sustainable enterprise and is an innovative, robust, and scalable ERP. We at Cloud ERP Product Success and Cloud Co-Innovation offer a service as versatile as our product itself. Check out the numerous offerings our team has created for you below:

entry.title: Demystifying Peppol’s Message Types: Transport Acknowledgement, MLR, BLR
entry.content:
Are you exchanging electronic documents with your business partners through the Peppol network using SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, cloud edition? If so, you have probably come across terms such as Transport Acknowledgement (Acks), Message Level Response (MLR) or Business Level Response (BLR). And you might already know that all three refer to types of messages that are exchanged in Peppol's 4-corner model. However, it might not be completely clear to you when and why each of these message types is sent, what information they convey, or how they look in the eDocument Cockpit or the Manage Electronic Documents app of your SAP S/4HANA, SAP ERP, or SAP S/4HANA Cloud system. With this blog, we would like to bring some clarity into these questions.

Additionally, we will talk about message types developed by SAP to update the status of the electronic document in the SAP business system. Even though, these message types are not part of the OpenPeppol specification, we are mentioning them for the sake of completeness of all message types in the process of sending an electronic document using SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, cloud edition.

 

Message Types

In general, you can think of the message types as a multi-stage check to ensure the reliable transmission of an electronic document over the Peppol network. Each message type confirms the status of a different stage in the transmission process.

Let's walk through the different message types using a concrete example. In the example, your business system is SAP S/4HANA Cloud, so the sender (corner 1) is represented by the Manage Electronic Documents app, and you are using SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, cloud edition (corner 2) to send electronic documents through the Peppol network to your business partners.

Example: Message types exchanged in Peppol’s 4-corner model when you send electronic documents

The starting point in the Peppol 4-corner model is the first corner where you, as the sender, submit an invoice in the Manage Electronic Documents app to your business partner. The document is then sent to the sender access point (corner 2) which is your SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, cloud edition - this is the corner where the first message type is created.

Message type: Confirmation from SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, Cloud Edition

Message Type: Confirmation from SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, Cloud Edition
This message type is not part of the message types defined by OpenPeppol but was developed by SAP as an additional layer to ensure that your document has securely and correctly reached the sender access point.

In the cloud edition (your sender access point), two things are checked:

1. Has the document arrived at the sender access point?

  • If yes, the cloud edition sends a message to your business system and the status in your Manage Electronic Documents app changes to Received by Sender Access Point.
  • If no, the cloud edition does not send a message to your business system and the status in your Manage Electronic Documents app stays in Sending Requested.

2. Does the document pass the validation checks? The validation checks involve comparing the document with the Schematron rules stored in the sender access point and making sure the receiving participant in the Peppol network can be identified by the sender access point. If the document doesn’t pass the validation checks, the cloud edition sends a message to your business system and the status in the app changes to Error at Sender Access Point.

To resolve errors that appear at this stage you can check the Troubleshooting guide or the SAP Help Portal.

Message type: Transport Acknowledgement (Acks)

Message type: Transport Acknowledgement (Acks)

When your document has been sent to the sender access point and has successfully passed validation there, the sender access point (corner 2) forwards the document to the receiver access point (corner 3). The receiver access point creates the next message type – the transport acknowledgement.

The forwarding of the document to the receiver access point as well as the transport acknowledgement is accomplished using the Applicability Statement 4 (AS4) messaging protocol. It is based on web services standards and is commonly used for the secure and reliable exchange of documents such as purchase orders, invoices, and shipping notices.

The message transfer in AS4 involves the following steps:

  1. Message Creation: The sender access point creates the message to be sent, which typically includes the actual document and any necessary metadata.
  2. Message Packaging: The message is then packaged according to the AS4 standard, which defines the structure and format of the message. The packaging includes adding headers, security information, and other necessary elements.
  3. Adding Digital Signature: After the message has been packaged, a digital signature is generated using the sender's private key. This digital signature is added to the message. This not only validates the sender’s identity, but also ensures the integrity of the message during transmission as it can be used to detect any changes made to the message after it has been signed.
  4. Message Transmission: The packaged message along with the digital signature is transmitted over the Peppol network, using HTTP protocols.
  5. Message Receipt: Once the receiver access point receives the message, it verifies the digital signature using the sender’s public key. If the verification is successful, it indicates that the message was not tampered with during transmission and was indeed sent by the claimed sender.
  6. Transport Acknowledgment: After the successful verification of the digital signature, the receiver access point sends back an acknowledgment to the sender access point to confirm the successful receipt of the message.

Once the transport acknowledgement reaches the cloud edition (corner 2), the cloud edition sends a message to your business system (corner 1) that triggers the status update in the Manage Electronic Documents app to Acknowledged by Receiver Access Point.

In the case of an unsuccessful message transfer using the AS4 protocol, such as a header error in the message, the receiver access point can’t send a transport acknowledgment to the sender access point. However, it's important to note that the cloud edition (corner 2) still ensures transparency in this scenario by sending a message to your business system, triggering a status change in the Manage Electronic Documents app to Rejected by Receiver Access Point.

Overall, AS4 ensures secure and reliable message transfer in B2B communication, and the use of digital signatures adds an additional layer of security and trust to the process.

Message type: Message Level Response (MLR)

Message type: Message Level Response (MLR)

The receiver access point performs another round of validations on the invoice, particularly focusing on the Schematron rules. Since there are multiple versions of these rules, the sender access point and the receiver access point might be checking against different versions. Hence, a document might pass the validation at the sender access point and fail at the receiver access point.

The message that can be sent by the receiver access point to inform about the validation status is referred to as Message Level Response (MLR). Let’s have a look at the scenarios:

  • You have enabled receiving MLR in the Configure Participants app of the cloud edition.
    • If the receiver access point supports the sending of MLR, you receive an MLR message with the information whether the receiver access point accepted or rejected the electronic document after the validation. In your Manage Electronic Documents app you’ll therefore see either the status Accepted by Rec. Acc. Point or Valid. Error at Rec. Acc Point.
    • If the receiver access point doesn’t support the sending of MLR, you won’t receive an MLR message. In your Manage Electronic Documents app you’ll therefore see the status Acknowl. by Rec. Acc. Point.
  • You haven’t enabled receiving MLR in the Configure Participants app of the cloud edition: Then of course you won’t receive an MLR message and in your Manage Electronic Documents app you’ll see the status Acknowl. by Rec. Acc. Point.

MLR messages contribute to the overall robustness, reliability, and traceability of the Peppol network by enabling businesses to ensure that their business messages have been successfully received and processed, and quickly identify and rectify issues.

For more information about the Message Level Response, check out this page in the help portal: Message Level Response

Message type: Business Level Response (BLR)

Message type: Business Level Response (BLR)

Finally, the receiver access point forwards the document to the receiver (corner 4). The receiver system, or the users themselves, can then decide whether they wish to communicate something to the sender. This means, BLR messages are optional and may not be sent by the receiver. This message, originating from the receiver, is called a Business Level Response (BLR), as it provides information on the business decision relating to the business document.

Unlike the MLR, which is an automated technical confirmation of the message receipt and processing, the BLR concerns the actual outcome of the business message.

The BLR message is forwarded through all the corners and depending on the BLR the statuses in the Manage Electronic Documents app can change. For example, if the original document is an invoice, the following statuses can appear:

  • In Process by Receiver
  • Under Query by Receiver
  • Conditionally Accepted by Rec.
  • Acknowledged by Receiver
  • Accepted by Receiver
  • Rejected by Receiver
  • Partially Paid by Receiver
  • Paid by Receiver

For more information about the Business Level Response, check out this page in the help portal: Business Level Response.

How to check all those messages in your system

You can trace all the messages from the beginning to the end of the exchange process in the Manage Electronic Documents app in your SAP S/4HANA Cloud system or in the eDocument Cockpit in your SAP ERP, SAP S/4HANA system.

In the Manage Electronic Documents app, expand the Details of the electronic document.

Screenshot of the Manage Electronic Documents app with focus on the Details button

In the Attachments section, you can choose Preview to view the messages.

Screenshot of the Manage Electronic Documents app with focus on the Attachments section and the Preview button

In the eDocument Cockpit, navigate to Goto > History, to access the History of the electronic document.

Screenshot of the eDocument Cockpit with focus on the History button

In the EDocument History view, you can scroll to the right until you see the File Name column. By double-clicking on the files you can view the messages.

Screenshot of the EDocument History view in the eDocument Cockpit with the focus on the File Name column

How to enable the message types

If you are in the latest support package for your release, all message types that we previously described are already enabled in your business system for all countries supported by SAP that exchange invoices over the Peppol network. For the sending of Business Level Response, we currently only support Invoice Responses with Accepted and Rejected status, with the exception of Singapore, where we also support Invoice Responses with Acknowledged status.

In the cloud edition however, you must activate the Message Types in the Configure Participants app. This can be done while setting up a participant for your company, by selecting the various message types under the Additional Document Types field. See Registering Your Company to Send Electronic Documents in the Peppol Network.

If you are not in the latest support package of your SAP S/4HANA or SAP ERP system, you can manually implement the following SAP Note: 2783795 - Electronic Document Processing for Peppol Countries/Regions: Implementation Overview.

Further information

For further information about the message types that are part of the OpenPeppol specification, check this page: BIS Message Level Response 3.0

For detailed information about the subcategory Invoice Response of the Business Level Response type, check this page: BIS Invoice Response 3.2

Give us Feedback!

Please share your feedback and thoughts in a comment. If you have questions about SAP Document Compliance, you can post your question here.

entry.title: Integrate Egypt E-Invoice with the Signing Server that hosts Cryptographic Token Device
entry.content:
Registration into the ETA Electronic Billing System. For more info https://www.eta.gov.eg/en/node/101

Apply to one of the entities licensed by ITIDA to provide digital signature/electronic seal services, which are mentioned in detail at this link.

Install an API Signing Solution on the In-house Signing Server that allows the signing processes through the SAP Cloud Integration. For more info https://www.eta.gov.eg/en/node/107 

Host the Cryptographic device (USB Token) in the In-house Signing Server and install the token driver application (e.g., epass2003).

Register the SAP backend system in the ETA portal to generate the necessary Client ID, Client Secret1, and Client Secret2. For more info Register an ERP system and follow the steps below:

  • Log in as a taxpayer delegate by using a valid username and password.
  • On the left-side menu, select Manage Profile, and then choose Taxpayer Profile.
  • Scroll down the page, and then go to Representatives.
  • On the upper-right corner, select + Register ERP.

Deploy the SAP Integration Package for Egypt Electronic Invoices in your SAP Integration Suite tenant,

  • Complete the essential configuration steps in the SAP API Hub’s guide for setting up your tenant in the SAP Integration Suite Please access.

Define and deploy all Secure Parameters in SAP Integration Suite Tenant to securely recognize and communicate the In-house Signing Server hosts a Cryptographic device (USB Token).

Security Parameter's fields :

  • Name[must] follows the specific format: edoc_egypt_<type>_<taxpayerid>. 
    • <type> must be one of the list below
    • <taxpayerid> Tax Registration Number must be without spaces and '-' character.
  • Description[text] parameter text description 
  • Secure Parameter[must] parameter value
  • Repeat Secure Parameter[must] parameter value

Security Parameter's mandatory types:

  1. pin: provided by the service provider with the USB Token device.
  2. thumbprint: included in the digital certificate issued by the service provider .
  3. secretkey: the License key of the signing middleware.
  4. signingServer: the IP address of the in-house signing server that hosts the USB Token device

Example of a Secure Parameter for type 'signingSever' :

  • Name: edoc_egypt_signingServer_123456789
  • Description: Signing server
  • Secure Parameter: Http://1.2.3.4:9999
  • Repeat Secure Parameter: Http://1.2.3.4:9999

Define OAuth2 Client Credentials in the SAP Integration Suite Tenant to securely authenticate the electronic invoice APIs and communicate with the ETA.

  • Name: follows the specified format: edoc_egypt_eta_<mode>_<taxpayerid>.
    The mode can be “test” or “prod” depending on the SAP integration suite tenant.
  • Token Service: The URL varies depending on the SAP Integration Suite Tenant that you choose to deploy.
    • Test tenant: https://id.preprod.eta.gov.eg/connect/token. 
    • Prod tenant: https://id.eta.gov.eg/connect/token
  • Client ID: generated when registering the SAP backend system.
  • Client Secret: generated when registering the SAP backend system.
  • Scope: Enter "InvoicingAPI"

 

Related blogs

  • Stay Compliant in Egypt with SAP Document and Repo... - SAP Community

entry.title: SAP Document and Reporting Compliance Brazil: Mass download of electronic fiscal documents
entry.content:
Versão em português aqui / Versión en español aquí 

Hi everyone,

The SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, outbound invoicing option for Brazil team has released a new functionality, the DF-e Mass Download.

Now, you can download many XML files of approved electronic fiscal documents (NF-e, CT-e, and NFS-e), including their events, for auditing purposes. The electronic fiscal document files are provided in a compressed file in tar.gz format.

SAP Document and Reporting Compliance: DF-e Mass Download

The new functionality delivers the following features:

  • The DF-e Mass Download (NFE_MASS_DOWNLOAD) transaction, or the DF-e Mass Download app for cloud users, provides several parameters that enable you to select the electronic fiscal documents according to your needs.

  • Download of many XML files from approved documents from specific company codes, business places, or CNPJ/CPF numbers and different electronic fiscal documents.

  • Better performance to download a high number of XML files.

  • Preview a summary of documents that will be downloaded in a specific group, classified by company codes, business places, and partners, based on filter selection.

  • The shortcut for the DF-e Mass Download (NFE_MASS_DOWNLOAD) transaction in the Goto menu of the NF-e/CT-e Monitor (J1BNFE) transaction.

  • Integration of the Usage Analytics dashboard with the downloaded files.

  • The NF-e/CT-e Monitor (J1BNFE) transaction is restricted to downloading less than 500 documents.

Implementation

You can implement these changes by following the instructions on the SAP Notes:

  • SAP Note 3493359
  • SAP Note 3493360

If you use the SAP S/4HANA or SAP ERP systems:

  • You can control access to the mass download functions using the Business Place Authorization Object for DF-e Mass Download (NFE_MBUPLA) authorization object. For further details regarding this object, refer to the authorization object documentation available within the system through the Authorization Object Maintenance (SU21) transaction.

  • You need to schedule the NF-e Mass Download Job Orchestrator (NFE_MASSD_JOB_REPORT) to run according to your business needs.

For more information about the DF-e Mass Download, refer to the following SAP Help Portal page:

Outbound Invoices > Mass Download

Integration with Usage Analytics

In the Usage Analytics dashboard, you find the mass downloaded files under Outbound Metric Name. In this metric, each group totals 15 thousand documents. Refer to the following scenarios as examples:

  • If you download 10 thousand documents, you will be charged for one group of 15 thousand documents.

  • If you download 25 thousand documents, you will be charged for one group of 15 thousand documents for the first 15 thousand documents and another group for the remaining 10 thousand documents.

  • If you download 50 thousand documents, you will be charged for four groups of 15 thousand documents each.

For more details about the Usage Analytics dashboard and the SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, outbound invoicing option for Brazil, you can check out the blog post explaining how to use this tool.

Related blog posts

  • How to Easily Browse the SAP Help Portal Pages for Outbound Nota Fiscal
  • How to Easily Browse the SAP Help Portal Pages for Inbound Nota Fiscal
  • An Introduction to SAP Document and Reporting Compliance: Navigating the SAP Help Portal

You can sign up for email alerts when new SAP Notes are released or updated in the components listed below; just follow the instructions in SAP Knowledge Base Article 2171560.

  • FI-LOC-NFE-BR-OUT
  • LOD-LH-NFE

Use the regulatory change manager tool to find out if a solution to a legal change is planned. Take a look at SAP Knowledge Base Article 3421995 and the regulatory change manager page to understand how to use this tool.

Have you found this blog post useful? If so, choose “like” (kudos), share it with your colleagues, and follow me for similar content.

You are welcome to leave your questions and read similar blog posts on the SAP Document and Reporting Compliance SAP Community. Don’t forget to follow the SAP Electronic Invoicing for Brazil (SAP Nota Fiscal Eletronica) tag and the SAP Document and Reporting Compliance community to keep up with the latest NF-e news.

Thank you, and until next time,
Thiago

entry.title: SAP Document and Reporting Compliance Brazil: Download em massa de documentos fiscais eletrônicos
entry.content:
English version here / Versión en español aquí 

Olá pessoal,

O time de SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, opção de faturamento de saída para o Brasil lançou uma nova funcionalidade, o DF-e Mass Download.

Agora, você pode baixar muitos arquivos XML de documentos fiscais eletrônicos aprovados (NF-e, CT-e, e NFS-e), incluindo seus eventos, para fins de auditoria. Os arquivos de documentos fiscais eletrônicos são fornecidos em um arquivo compactado no formato tar.gz.

SAP Document and Reporting Compliance: DF-e Mass Download

 

A nova funcionalidade oferece os seguintes recursos:

  • A transação DF-e Mass Download (NFE_MASS_DOWNLOAD), ou o app DF-e Mass Download para usuários da nuvem, fornece vários parâmetros que permitem selecionar os documentos fiscais eletrônicos de acordo com suas necessidades.

  • Download de muitos arquivos XML de documentos aprovados de empresas específicas, locais de negócios ou números de CNPJ/CPF e diferentes documentos fiscais eletrônicos.

  • Melhor desempenho para baixar vários arquivos XML.

  • Visualização de um resumo dos documentos que serão baixados em um grupo específico, classificados por empresas, locais de negócios e parceiros, com base na seleção do filtro.

  • Atalho para a transação DF-e Mass Download (NFE_MASS_DOWNLOAD) no menu Ir Para da transação Monitor NF-e/CT-e (J1BNFE).

  • Integração do dashboard Usage Analytics com os arquivos baixados.

  • Transação Monitor NF-e/CT-e (J1BNFE) restrita a baixar menos de 500 documentos.

Implementação

Você pode implementar essas alterações seguindo as instruções nas SAP Notes:

  • SAP Note 3493359
  • SAP Note 3493360

Se você usa sistemas SAP S/4HANA ou SAP ERP:

  • Você pode controlar o acesso às funções de download em massa usando o objeto de autorização Business Place Authorization Object for DF-e Mass Download (NFE_MBUPLA). Para mais detalhes sobre esse objeto, consulte a documentação do objeto de autorização disponível no sistema por meio da transação Atualizar Objetos de Autorização (SU21).

  • Você deve agendar o job NF-e Mass Download Job Orchestrator (NFE_MASSD_JOB_REPORT) para ser executado de acordo com suas necessidades comerciais.

Para obter mais informações sobre o DF-e Mass Download, consulte a seguinte página do portal SAP Help:

Outbound Invoices > Mass Download

Integração com o Usage Analytics

No dashboard do Usage Analytics, você encontra os arquivos baixados em massa em Outbound Metric Name. Nessa métrica, cada grupo totaliza 15 mil documentos. Consulte os seguintes cenários como exemplos:

  • Se você baixar 10 mil documentos, será cobrado por um grupo de 15 mil documentos.

  • Se você baixar 25 mil documentos, será cobrado por um grupo de 15 mil documentos para os primeiros 15 mil documentos e outro grupo para os 10 mil documentos restantes.

  • Se você baixar 50 mil documentos, será cobrado por quatro grupos de 15 mil documentos cada.

Para mais detalhes sobre o dashboard do Usage Analytics e SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, opção de emissão de faturamento de saída para o Brasil, você pode conferir o blog post que explica como usar essa ferramenta.

Blog posts relacionados

  • Como navegar facilmente nas páginas do SAP Help Portal para Outbound Nota Fiscal
  • Como navegar facilmente nas páginas do SAP Help Portal para Inbound Nota Fiscal
  • An Introduction to SAP Document and Reporting Compliance: Navigating the SAP Help Portal

Você pode receber alerta por email quando novas SAP Notes forem lançadas ou atualizadas nos componentes listados abaixo; basta seguir as instruções do SAP Knowledge Base Article 2171560.

  • FI-LOC-NFE-BR-OUT
  • LOD-LH-NFE

Acesse o gerenciador de modificações legais para ter uma noção do que está planejado para ser suportado. Consulte o SAP Knowledge Base Article 3421995 e a página Regulatory Change Manager para entender como usar esta ferramenta.

Gostou desse post? Dê um “like” (kudos) e compartilhe o conteúdo com seus colegas. Sinta-se à vontade para deixar suas perguntas no SAP Community e ler posts semelhantes no blogging space do SAP Document and Reporting Compliance.

Para acompanhar as últimas notícias da NF-e, siga a tag SAP Electronic Invoicing for Brazil (SAP Nota Fiscal Eletronica) e a comunidade SAP Document and Reporting Compliance.

Obrigado e até a próxima,
Thiago

entry.title: SAP Document and Reporting Compliance Brazil: Descarga masiva de documentos fiscales electrónicos
entry.content:
English version here / Versão em português aqui

 ¡Hola a todos!

El equipo de SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, outbound invoicing option for Brazil ha lanzado una nueva funcionalidad, el DF-e Mass Download.

Ahora, puede descargar una gran cantidad archivos XML de documentos fiscales electrónicos aprobados (NF-e, CT-e, y NFS-e), incluidos sus eventos, para fines de auditoría. Los archivos de documentos fiscales electrónicos se proporcionan en un archivo comprimido en formato tar.gz.

SAP Document and Reporting Compliance: DF-e Mass Download

 

La nueva funcionalidad ofrece los siguientes recursos:

  • La transacción DF-e Mass Download (NFE_MASS_DOWNLOAD), o la app DF-e Mass Download para usuarios de cloud, proporciona varios parámetros que le permiten seleccionar los documentos fiscales electrónicos según sus necesidades.

  • Descarga de muchos archivos XML de documentos aprobados de sociedades, lugares de negocio, números de CNPJ/CPF, y diferentes documentos fiscales electrónicos.

  • Mejor desempeño para descargar una gran cantidad de archivos XML.

  • Vista previa de un resumen de los documentos que se descargarán en un grupo específico, clasificados por sociedades, lugares de negocio e interlocutores comerciales (partners), según la selección de filtros.

  • El acceso directo para la transacción de DF-e Mass Download (NFE_MASS_DOWNLOAD) en el menú Ir a de la transacción de NF-e/CT-e Monitor (J1BNFE).

  • Integración del dashboard Usage Analytics con los archivos descargados.

  • Transacción de NF-e/CT-e Monitor (J1BNFE) restringida a la descarga de menos de 500 documentos.


Implementación

Puede implementar estos cambios siguiendo las instrucciones de las SAP Notes:

  • SAP Note 3493359
  • SAP Note 3493360

Si utiliza los sistemas SAP S/4HANA o SAP ERP:

  • Puede controlar el acceso a las funciones de descarga masiva mediante el objeto de autorización Business Place Authorization Object for DF-e Mass Download (NFE_MBUPLA). Para obtener más detalles sobre este objeto, consulte la documentación del objeto de autorización disponible dentro del sistema a través de la transacción Actualización de Objeto de Autorización (SU21).

  • Debe agendar el NF-e Mass Download Job Orchestrator (NFE_MASSD_JOB_REPORT) para que se ejecute de acuerdo con las necesidades de tu negocio.

Para obtener más información sobre el DF-e Mass Download, consulte la siguiente página del portal SAP Help:

Outbound Invoices > Mass Download

Integración con Usage Analytics

En el dashboard de Usage Analytics, encontrará los archivos descargados en masa en Outbound Metric Name. En esta métrica, cada grupo suma 15 mil documentos. Consulte los siguientes escenarios como ejemplos:

  • Si descarga 10 mil documentos, se le cobrará por un grupo de 15 mil documentos.

  • Si descarga 25 mil documentos, se le cobrará por un grupo de 15 mil documentos por los primeros 15 mil documentos y por otro grupo por los 10 mil documentos restantes.

  • Si descarga 50 mil documentos, se le cobrará por cuatro grupos de 15 mil documentos cada uno.

Para obtener más detalles sobre el dashboard de Usage Analytics y SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, outbound invoicing option for Brazil, puede consultar la publicación que explica cómo usar esta herramienta.

Publicaciones relacionadas

  • How to Easily Browse the SAP Help Portal Pages for Outbound Nota Fiscal
  • How to Easily Browse the SAP Help Portal Pages for Inbound Nota Fiscal
  • An Introduction to SAP Document and Reporting Compliance: Navigating the SAP Help Portal

Puede registrarse para recibir alertas por correo electrónico cuando se publiquen o actualicen nuevas SAP Notes en los componentes que se enumeran a continuación; solo siga las instrucciones del SAP Knowledge Base Article 2171560.

  • FI-LOC-NFE-BR-OUT
  • LOD-LH-NFE

Use la herramienta regulatory change manager para averiguar si se planea una solución para un cambio legal. Siga las instrucciones del SAP Knowledge Base Article 3421995 y a la página regulatory change manager para usar esta herramienta.

¿Te ha parecido útil este blog? Si es así, haga clic en “like” (kudos), compártelo con tus colegas y sígueme para ver contenido similar. No dude en dejar sus preguntas en la comunidad SAP Document and Reporting Compliance. Y lea publicaciones de blog similares en blogging space SAP Document and Reporting Compliance.

No olvide seguir la tag SAP Electronic Invoicing for Brazil (SAP Nota Fiscal Eletronica) y la comunidad SAP Document and Reporting Compliance para tener conocimiento de las últimas noticias de NF-e.

Gracias y saludos,
Thiago
